chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the framework source code in Reference [24] (https://github.com/runphi). It also details the implementation of the building tool and image manager scripts within Section IV-C.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation utilizes both emulated environments (QEMU) for early testing and real hardware deployment on a Xilinx Kria KV260 SoC featuring RPUs. This constitutes a physical testbed with heterogeneous hardware, satisfying the highest rigor criteria.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section V-C ('Isolation from Failures') contains a dedicated experiment where the authors explicitly define the fault model as a 'kernel panic' injected into the Linux kernel (PVM) via '/proc/sysrq-trigger'. They formally demonstrate that ZICs survive this specific failure while standard containers do not.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs. Section V-A quantifies boot times with statistical distributions and compares them to Linux containers. Section V-B analyzes temporal isolation under stress (activation latency/response time) using TACLeBench suites, providing concrete metrics on performance overhead vs. isolation guarantees.

major_weakness: The paper does not explicitly quantify the specific CPU or memory overhead introduced by the runPHI runtime and partitioning hypervisor compared to standard Linux containers in terms of absolute resource consumption percentages.
